item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. The author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

Not really a whole lot to add to this. Its a relatively contained story with a good continuation in the GoI format already linked at the bottom. Really the only thing that wouldn't be intrusive, or require a contrived addition to the article would be an inline link to the MC&D Hub As such, I am suggesting the following:

Editing this:

SCP-2418 was acquired in 2011 as a part of Operation Harpocrates, a coordinated attack on Marshall, Carter & Dark assets in Egypt, during which the Foundation used a combination of physical raids, political upheaval and economic manipulation to effectively remove the presence of MC&D and associated groups from the country.

To this:

SCP-2418 was acquired in 2011 as a part of Operation Harpocrates, a coordinated attack on Marshall, Carter & Dark assets in Egypt, during which the Foundation used a combination of physical raids, political upheaval and economic manipulation to effectively remove the presence of MC&D and associated groups from the country.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. I don't know what it looked like before the edits but I definitely enjoyed the Roman flavor injected into this article. Gave it a very authentic historical feel. Never thought I would see Antinoüs' fate rendered in an SCP. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wish I'd gotten a chance to see this after you made the edits. Now the interview actually has flavor, and it's absolutely an improvement. Still not enough for me to upvote, though — if the attached document was good I'd be totally willing to, but I've already elaborated upon my problems with that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is absolutely reasonable. I'm tweaking the Roman documentation so it's not just "SCP Format But Old". I might also add a rental record for the MCD document, like you mentioned in that thread; I see what you mean about it being a lot of history without a lot of hook.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Looks like we found another instance of SCP-19316-J. I mean really, that's the most creative way you could come up with for the Romans to document something?

No particular thoughts about this otherwise, but I've seen MC&D documentation turn otherwise unspectacular items into a story worth telling, so I'm optimistic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I went and looked up the history and mythology behind this and was duly rewarded. Good stuff!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm all in for Ancient Roman Foundation stuff. The tie in with MC&D was also a very nice touch.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There was a rumor in the mid-1st century that the Emperor Hadrian's boyfriend Antinoüs had died not by drowning when he fell off a boat in the Nile, but in a sacrifice to maintain the Emperor's health. This is about that.
